session_b_findings.finding: Registry 135 shows a consistent human-divine mirroring structure: nearly every human inner-being act (turning, relenting, comforting, recalling) has a divine counterpart in the same registry (God relenting, God comforting, God withholding wrath). Session B should analyse this pairing as a key structural feature of the repentance domain in Scripture.

H4941H mish.pat appears in both Reg 24 (condemnation) and Reg 98 (justice) — three groups each. Session B must read both registries' mish.pat groups together to form a complete inner-being profile of this term.

The conscience (suneidesis/sunoida) groups in Reg 26 show a consistent pattern: moral self-awareness operating under divine authority — conscience as inner witness that remains subordinate to God's judgment. Session B should examine the relationship between conscience-as-moral-witness and divine judgment as the higher court.

Reg 35 contains two analytically distinct sub-clusters: (1) willing-spirit/eager-disposition (prothumia, prothumos, prothumōs, euthumeō, euthumos, athumeō) and (2) covetous-desire proper (pleonexia). Session B should treat these as distinct inner-being phenomena requiring separate analytical attention.

Reg 50 shows disobedience operating across three distinct inner-being dimensions in only 4 groups: Character/Disposition (defining orientation), Spiritual/God-ward (self-exclusion from divine rest), and Moral/Conscience (covenantal consequence). Session B should examine whether disobedience is primarily a dispositional, relational, or moral-act category in Scripture.

G0726 harpazō appears in two groups with contrasting inner-being engagements: [5493-001] predatory violation of inner content (Spiritual/God-ward) and [5493-002] divine protective snatching (Theological/Divine-Human). Session B should examine whether these two uses require distinct analytical treatment or represent a unified category of inner-being vulnerability and security.

The guilt registry (Reg 73) shows Spiritual/God-ward as the dominant dimension — sin and guilt are primarily framed in Scripture as relational conditions before God rather than psychological states. Session B analysis should proceed from this orientation: guilt is fundamentally a God-ward condition, with Moral/Conscience and Affective/Emotional dimensions secondary.

G4893 suneidesis in Reg 73 shows a four-dimensional conscience profile: (1) moral witness [53-001], (2) vulnerability [53-002], (3) interior foundation [53-003], (4) object of purification [53-004]. Session B should analyse these four functions as a coherent inner-being anatomy of conscience.

H4941G mish.pat (Reg 98) and H4941H mish.pat (Reg 24) are the same Hebrew term across two registries: five groups in Reg 98 and three groups in Reg 24 constitute the full inner-being profile. Session B must read both registries' mish.pat groups together for a complete analysis.

The dikaios/dikaiosunē/dikaiōsis/dikaioō family (G1342, G1343, G1344, G1347) shows a consistent tension: righteousness as divine act/attribute (Theological/Divine-Human) vs righteousness as human inner quality (Moral/Conscience). This is one of the most analytically significant tensions in the programme. Session B must examine this carefully.

H6664G tsedeq and H6663 tsadeq produce four distinct groups: (1) human inner quality [942-001], (2) divine attribute [942-002], (3) pursued inner orientation [942-003], (4) moral basis for governance [942-004]. This fourfold pattern in a single Hebrew root is a major Session B analytical focus.

session_d_pointers.description: The divine-relenting vocabulary (na.cham, shuv divine pole) in Reg 135 is closely related to the divine-judging vocabulary in Reg 98 and the condemnation/no-condemnation polarity in Reg 24. Session D should examine whether divine relenting, divine withholding of condemnation, and divine justification constitute a unified inner-being arc — the inner person moving from guilt through repentance to freedom.

The polarity structure of condemnation (verdict/no-verdict) is the governing framework of Reg 24 and maps onto the no-condemnation freedom of Romans 8. Session D should examine the condemnation/no-condemnation polarity as the judicial frame within which the guilt-repentance-justification arc operates across C13.

Reg 26 includes G4267 proginōskō (Identity/Selfhood) alongside conscience terms — this brings together the epistemic cluster (knowing, perception) with the identity-constituting dimension of divine foreknowing. Session D should examine whether being-known-by-God and knowing-oneself-morally form a coherent inner-being pairing that constitutes a distinct dimension of conscience.

Registry 30 (contrition) produces strong convergence: 5 of 9 groups assign Dependence / Creatureliness (7552-003, 7553-001, 7554-001, 7555-001, 7557-001). This convergence suggests contrition is the penitential sub-form of Dependence / Creatureliness — brokenness before God arising from moral self-awareness and recognition of unworthiness. Session D should examine whether the programme vocabulary needs a finer distinction between general dependence/trust and specifically penitential dependence (contrition). Connect with Reg 10 (dependence/trust) and Reg 80 (trust) in the programme.

The positive desire vocabulary (prothumia, prothumos, prothumōs) in Reg 35 may have natural affinity with volitional vocabulary elsewhere. The contrast between eager willing (prothumia) and acquisitive desire (pleonexia) in the same registry is analytically significant for understanding how Scripture distinguishes rightly-oriented from wrongly-oriented inner desire. Session D should examine this alongside Reg 70 (greed) and the broader desire vocabulary.

The greed vocabulary in C13 (pleonexia, pleonektēs, harpagē) shows consistent convergence on Character/Disposition. Session D should examine the relationship between greed (Reg 70), covetousness (Reg 35), and positive desire (prothumia) — the cluster's contrast between rightly- and wrongly-directed inner desire is a synthesis question.

The inner-being movement from guilt-incurred (Reg 73) through repentance (Reg 135) to freedom from condemnation (Reg 24) and justification (Reg 98) constitutes the governing arc of C13. Session D should examine whether this four-registry trajectory (guilt → repentance → no-condemnation → justification) maps a complete inner-being soteriology visible in Scripture.

G0266 hamartia produces three dimension-distinct groups: 9-001 (sin as moral condition — Moral Character/HUMAN), 9-002 (sin as indwelling power — Agency/Power/UNSEEN), 9-003 (sin as universal condition — Moral Character/HUMAN). The shift from HUMAN to UNSEEN subject within a single lexeme is analytically significant — the same word names both the person's moral condition and an agency distinct from the person. This is unusual dimension-splitting within a single term and warrants cross-registry Session D analysis. Compare with other terms functioning as both condition and agent (death, flesh, law in Pauline literature).

Reg 98 (justice) shows repeated convergence between inner moral quality (Moral/Conscience) and divine action (Theological/Divine-Human) — particularly in justification/righteousness terminology. Session D should examine the relationship between the justice cluster and the condemnation registry (Reg 24) as structurally complementary: condemnation and justification are the negative and positive poles of the same judicial framework.

Two groups in Reg 98 — [3186-001] H0197H ulam (Hall of Justice) and [3188-001] H0197I ulam (Hall of Throne) — are QA-EXTERNALISED: they describe physical structures. Session D should examine the question of institutional/architectural embodiment of inner commitment to justice — to what extent does the justice vocabulary in Scripture include structural referents, and what is their inner-being significance.

G1097 ginōskō has 824 occurrences and 120 verses in export. Only a conscience-relevant subset has been analysed. Full corpus is far beyond Session B scope. Phase 2 independent research required before synthesis-level conclusions drawn from this term's full range.
G1492H eidō (see/perceive) has 396 occurrences and 119 verses in export. Only conscience-morally-relevant subset analysed. Phase 2 required.
G1492I eidō (know/understand) has 365 occurrences and 27 verses in export. Conscience-morally-relevant subset only. Phase 2 required.

H3627 ke.li has 325 occurrences and 225 verses in export. Classified extracted under broad-sweep approach for vessel/container metaphor relevance. The majority of the corpus is literal (weapons, bags, utensils) — not conscience vocabulary. Analysis must be confined strictly to the vessel-as-person / consecrated-vessel theological register. Phase 2 review required before any synthesis-level use of this term.

G1921 epiginōskō has 136 occurrences and 39 verses in export. Conscience-relevant subset (recognition, moral self-examination, spiritual perception) is a minority of the full corpus. Phase 2 required for full assessment.

Condemnation (katakrima/katakrinō family) is a candidate for a new registry entry. G2631 katakrima and root family G2632 katakrinō (to condemn), katakrisis, akatakritos, autokatakritos constitute a distinct inner-being vocabulary cluster addressing the judicial-moral verdict-state. Analytically distinct from guilt (moral fact of wrongdoing) and shame (relational/exposure experience). The condemnation/no-condemnation polarity is the structural framework of Romans 5-8 and is signalled by anchor verse Rom 8:1. ACTION: Check word_registry for existing condemnation entry. If absent, create new registry with anchor verse Rom 8:1 and run Session A extraction on G2632 family.

G0264 hamartanō (to sin, occ=241) has only 37 verses in the export — 15% coverage. Synthesis-level conclusions about the verbal form of sin in the NT cannot be drawn from this sample alone. Full verse set required before Phase 2 synthesis.

G0265 hamartēma (sin as discrete act, occ=26) has only 4 verses in the export — 15% coverage. The distinction between hamartia (sin as state/principle) and hamartēma (sin as individual act) is analytically significant. Conclusions about this distinction require Phase 2 full-verse verification.
G0093 (unrighteousness) — 214 occurrences, 24 verses in export (11% coverage). Analysis draws on available sample only. Full verse set required before synthesis-level conclusions on this term.
G0094 (unjust) — 101 occurrences, 11 verses in export (11% coverage). Analysis draws on available sample only. Full verse set required before synthesis-level conclusions on this term.
G0095 (unjustly) — 22 occurrences, 1 verses in export (5% coverage). Analysis draws on available sample only. Full verse set required before synthesis-level conclusions on this term.
G1342 (just) — 383 occurrences, 74 verses in export (19% coverage). Analysis draws on available sample only. Full verse set required before synthesis-level conclusions on this term.
G1345 (righteous act) — 128 occurrences, 10 verses in export (8% coverage). Analysis draws on available sample only. Full verse set required before synthesis-level conclusions on this term.
G1349 (condemnation) — 21 occurrences, 3 verses in export (14% coverage). Analysis draws on available sample only. Full verse set required before synthesis-level conclusions on this term.
G1556 (to avenge) — 71 occurrences, 6 verses in export (8% coverage). Analysis draws on available sample only. Full verse set required before synthesis-level conclusions on this term.
G1557 (vengeance) — 59 occurrences, 9 verses in export (15% coverage). Analysis draws on available sample only. Full verse set required before synthesis-level conclusions on this term.
G2118 (righteousness) — 21 occurrences, 1 verses in export (5% coverage). Analysis draws on available sample only. Full verse set required before synthesis-level conclusions on this term.
G2917 (judgment) — 232 occurrences, 27 verses in export (12% coverage). Analysis draws on available sample only. Full verse set required before synthesis-level conclusions on this term.
G2920 (judgment) — 253 occurrences, 47 verses in export (19% coverage). Analysis draws on available sample only. Full verse set required before synthesis-level conclusions on this term.
H4639H (deed: justice) — classified extracted_thin on sub-gloss label only. NO_VERSES in export. Independent verse research required before any synthesis conclusions drawn from this term.
H4639I (deed: judgement) — classified extracted_thin on sub-gloss label only. NO_VERSES in export. Independent verse research required before any synthesis conclusions drawn from this term.

H6419 (pālal, to pray, 84 occ) and H8605 (tepillāh, prayer, 77 occ) classified xref_prayer. Both carry a judicial-intercession dimension — prayer as address to the divine judge — directly relevant to justice. Session D must assess whether the prayer registry analysis adequately captures this usage, and if not, initiate a cross-registry pass.
